ayahEnglish: And they found a servant from among Our servants [i.e., al-Khiḍr] to whom We had given mercy from Us and had taught him from Us a [certain] knowledge.

ayahArabic: فَوَجَدَا عَبْدًا مِّنْ عِبَادِنَآ ءَاتَيْنٰهُ رَحْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِنَا وَعَلَّمْنٰهُ مِن لَّدُنَّا عِلْمًا
ayahEnglish: And they found a servant from among Our servants [i.e., al-Khiḍr] to whom We had given mercy from Us and had taught him from Us a [certain] knowledge.

targetTranslation: knowledge (object of عَلَّمْنٰهُ - we taught him)
ayahArabic: فَوَجَدَا عَبْدًا مِّنْ عِبَادِنَآ ءَاتَيْنٰهُ رَحْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِنَا وَعَلَّمْنٰهُ مِن لَّدُنَّا عِلْمًا
ayahEnglish: And they found a servant from among Our servants [i.e., al-Khiḍr] to whom We had given mercy from Us and had taught him from Us a [certain] knowledge.

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُۥ مُوسَىٰ هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْدًا
ayahEnglish: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُۥ مُوسَىٰ هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْدًا
ayahEnglish: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُۥ مُوسَىٰ هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْدًا
ayahEnglish: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"

targetTranslation: you teach me (Musa asking Khidr to teach him)

ayahEnglish: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"

ayahEnglish: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"

targetTranslation: right guidance (object of تُعَلِّمَنِ - teach me)
ayahArabic: قَالَ لَهُۥ مُوسَىٰ هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْدًا
ayahEnglish: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Indeed, with me you will never be able to have patience.

ayahArabic: قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Indeed, with me you will never be able to have patience.

targetTranslation: with me (referring to Moses speaking to Khidr)
ayahArabic: قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Indeed, with me you will never be able to have patience.

targetTranslation: patience (object of تَسْتَطِيعَ - you will be able to)
ayahArabic: قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Indeed, with me you will never be able to have patience.

ayahArabic: وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلَىٰ مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِۦ خُبْرًا
ayahEnglish: And how can you have patience for what you do not encompass in knowledge?"

ayahArabic: وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلَىٰ مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِۦ خُبْرًا
ayahEnglish: And how can you have patience for what you do not encompass in knowledge?"

targetTranslation: you/she encompass (in negative context of لَمْ)
ayahArabic: وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلَىٰ مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِۦ خُبْرًا
ayahEnglish: And how can you have patience for what you do not encompass in knowledge?"

targetTranslation: knowledge/information (object of تُحِطْ - you did not encompass)
ayahArabic: وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلَىٰ مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِۦ خُبْرًا
ayahEnglish: And how can you have patience for what you do not encompass in knowledge?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ صَابِرًا وَلَآ أَعْصِى لَكَ أَمْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "You will find me, if Allāh wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in [any] order."

ayahArabic: قَالَ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ صَابِرًا وَلَآ أَعْصِى لَكَ أَمْرًا

ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "You will find me, if Allāh wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in [any] order."

targetTranslation: patient (object of سَتَجِدُنِي - you will find me)

ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "You will find me, if Allāh wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in [any] order."

ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "You will find me, if Allāh wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in [any] order."

ayahArabic: قَالَ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ صَابِرًا وَلَآ أَعْصِى لَكَ أَمْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "You will find me, if Allāh wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in [any] order."

ayahArabic: قَالَ فَإِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَنِى فَلَا تَسْـَٔلْنِى عَن شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰٓ أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention [i.e., explanation]."

ayahArabic: قَالَ فَإِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَنِى فَلَا تَسْـَٔلْنِى عَن شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰٓ أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention [i.e., explanation]."

ayahEnglish: He said, "Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention [i.e., explanation]."

targetTranslation: it was brought up (as a topic)
ayahArabic: قَالَ فَإِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَنِى فَلَا تَسْـَٔلْنِى عَن شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰٓ أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention [i.e., explanation]."

ayahArabic: قَالَ فَإِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَنِى فَلَا تَسْـَٔلْنِى عَن شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰٓ أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention [i.e., explanation]."

ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَكِبَا فِى ٱلسَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَهَا قَالَ أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا إِمْرًا
ayahEnglish: So they set out, until when they had embarked on the ship, he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] tore it open. [Moses] said, "Have you torn it open to drown its people? You have certainly done a grave thing."

targetTranslation: they both rode (referring to Moses and Al-Khidr boarding the ship)
ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَكِبَا فِى ٱلسَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَهَا قَالَ أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا إِمْرًا
ayahEnglish: So they set out, until when they had embarked on the ship, he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] tore it open. [Moses] said, "Have you torn it open to drown its people? You have certainly done a grave thing."

targetTranslation: he made a hole in it (acted on)

ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَكِبَا فِى ٱلسَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَهَا قَالَ أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا إِمْرًا
ayahEnglish: So they set out, until when they had embarked on the ship, he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] tore it open. [Moses] said, "Have you torn it open to drown its people? You have certainly done a grave thing."

ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَكِبَا فِى ٱلسَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَهَا قَالَ أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا إِمْرًا

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Al-Khiḍr] said, "Did I not say that with me you would never be able to have patience?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا

ayahEnglish: [Al-Khiḍr] said, "Did I not say that with me you would never be able to have patience?"

targetTranslation: you are able (after لَن meaning 'will not' in this ayah)

ayahArabic: قَالَ لَا تُؤَاخِذْنِى بِمَا نَسِيتُ وَلَا تُرْهِقْنِى مِنْ أَمْرِى عُسْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "Do not blame me for what I forgot and do not overwhelm me in my matter with difficulty."

targetTranslation: you hold me responsible (connected to لَا to mean 'do not hold me responsible')
ayahArabic: قَالَ لَا تُؤَاخِذْنِى بِمَا نَسِيتُ وَلَا تُرْهِقْنِى مِنْ أَمْرِى عُسْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "Do not blame me for what I forgot and do not overwhelm me in my matter with difficulty."

ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "Do not blame me for what I forgot and do not overwhelm me in my matter with difficulty."

targetTranslation: any hardship (as the object being acted upon in تُرْهِقْنِى)
ayahArabic: قَالَ لَا تُؤَاخِذْنِى بِمَا نَسِيتُ وَلَا تُرْهِقْنِى مِنْ أَمْرِى عُسْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "Do not blame me for what I forgot and do not overwhelm me in my matter with difficulty."

targetTranslation: when (followed by لَقِيَا - they both met)
ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا لَقِيَا غُلٰمًا فَقَتَلَهُۥ قَالَ أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْسًا زَكِيَّةًۢ بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ لَّقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا نُّكْرًا
ayahEnglish: So they set out, until when they met a boy, he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] killed him. [Moses] said, "Have you killed a pure soul for other than [having killed] a soul? You have certainly done a deplorable thing."

targetTranslation: they both met (referring to Moses and Al-Khidr in the story)

ayahArabic: قَالَ إِن سَأَلْتُكَ عَن شَىْءٍۭ بَعْدَهَا فَلَا تُصٰحِبْنِى قَدْ بَلَغْتَ مِن لَّدُنِّى عُذْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Moses] said, "If I should ask you about anything after this, then do not keep me as a companion. You have obtained from me an excuse."

ayahArabic: قَالَ إِن سَأَلْتُكَ عَن شَىْءٍۭ بَعْدَهَا فَلَا تُصٰحِبْنِى قَدْ بَلَغْتَ مِن لَّدُنِّى عُذْرًا

ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَتَيَآ أَهْلَ قَرْيَةٍ ٱسْتَطْعَمَآ أَهْلَهَا فَأَبَوْا۟ أَن يُضَيِّفُوهُمَا فَوَجَدَا فِيهَا جِدَارًا يُرِيدُ أَن يَنقَضَّ فَأَقَامَهُۥ قَالَ لَوْ شِئْتَ لَتَّخَذْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا
ayahEnglish: So they set out, until when they came to the people of a town, they asked its people for food, but they refused to offer them hospitality. And they found therein a wall about to collapse, so he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] restored it. [Moses] said, "If you wished, you could have taken for it a payment."

ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَتَيَآ أَهْلَ قَرْيَةٍ ٱسْتَطْعَمَآ أَهْلَهَا فَأَبَوْا۟ أَن يُضَيِّفُوهُمَا فَوَجَدَا فِيهَا جِدَارًا يُرِيدُ أَن يَنقَضَّ فَأَقَامَهُۥ قَالَ لَوْ شِئْتَ لَتَّخَذْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا

ayahArabic: قَالَ هٰذَا فِرَاقُ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنِكَ سَأُنَبِّئُكَ بِتَأْوِيلِ مَا لَمْ تَسْتَطِع عَّلَيْهِ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: [Al-Khiḍr] said, "This is parting between me and you. I will inform you of the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience.

targetTranslation: they work/act/do (referring to the poor people who own the boat)
ayahArabic: أَمَّا ٱلسَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسٰكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ فَأَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَعِيبَهَا وَكَانَ وَرَآءَهُم مَّلِكٌ يَأْخُذُ كُلَّ سَفِينَةٍ غَصْبًا
ayahEnglish: As for the ship, it belonged to poor people working at sea. So I intended to cause defect in it as there was after them a king who seized every [good] ship by force.

targetTranslation: I damage it (referring to السَّفِينَةُ - the ship)

ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلْغُلٰمُ فَكَانَ أَبَوَاهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ فَخَشِينَآ أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا طُغْيٰنًا وَكُفْرًا
ayahEnglish: And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief.

targetTranslation: two believers (describing the parents in أَبَوَاهُ)
ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلْغُلٰمُ فَكَانَ أَبَوَاهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ فَخَشِينَآ أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا طُغْيٰنًا وَكُفْرًا
ayahEnglish: And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief.

ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلْغُلٰمُ فَكَانَ أَبَوَاهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ فَخَشِينَآ أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا طُغْيٰنًا وَكُفْرًا
ayahEnglish: And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief.

targetTranslation: into rebellion (object of يُرْهِقَهُمَا - burden them)
ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلْغُلٰمُ فَكَانَ أَبَوَاهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ فَخَشِينَآ أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا طُغْيٰنًا وَكُفْرًا
ayahEnglish: And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief.

targetTranslation: he exchanges/will exchange them both (referring to the parents exchanging their son)
ayahArabic: فَأَرَدْنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَوٰةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا
ayahEnglish: So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.

ayahArabic: فَأَرَدْنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَوٰةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا
ayahEnglish: So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.

targetTranslation: something better (object of يُبْدِلَهُمَا - replace them with)
ayahArabic: فَأَرَدْنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَوٰةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا
ayahEnglish: So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.

ayahArabic: فَأَرَدْنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَوٰةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا
ayahEnglish: So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.

ayahArabic: فَأَرَدْنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَوٰةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا
ayahEnglish: So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.

ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلْجِدَارُ فَكَانَ لِغُلٰمَيْنِ يَتِيمَيْنِ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ وَكَانَ تَحْتَهُۥ كَنزٌ لَّهُمَا وَكَانَ أَبُوهُمَا صٰلِحًا فَأَرَادَ رَبُّكَ أَن يَبْلُغَآ أَشُدَّهُمَا وَيَسْتَخْرِجَا كَنزَهُمَا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَمَا فَعَلْتُهُۥ عَنْ أَمْرِى ذٰلِكَ تَأْوِيلُ مَا لَمْ تَسْطِع عَّلَيْهِ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and there was beneath it a treasure for them, and their father had been righteous. So your Lord intended that they reach maturity and extract their treasure, as a mercy from your Lord. And I did it not of my own accord. That is the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience."

targetTranslation: under/beneath it (referring to الْجِدَارُ - the wall)
ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلْجِدَارُ فَكَانَ لِغُلٰمَيْنِ يَتِيمَيْنِ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ وَكَانَ تَحْتَهُۥ كَنزٌ لَّهُمَا وَكَانَ أَبُوهُمَا صٰلِحًا فَأَرَادَ رَبُّكَ أَن يَبْلُغَآ أَشُدَّهُمَا وَيَسْتَخْرِجَا كَنزَهُمَا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَمَا فَعَلْتُهُۥ عَنْ أَمْرِى ذٰلِكَ تَأْوِيلُ مَا لَمْ تَسْطِع عَّلَيْهِ صَبْرًا
ayahEnglish: And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and there was beneath it a treasure for them, and their father had been righteous. So your Lord intended that they reach maturity and extract their treasure, as a mercy from your Lord. And I did it not of my own accord. That is the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience."

targetTranslation: possessor of (connected to الْقَرْنَيْنِ - the two horns)
ayahArabic: وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَن ذِى ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ قُلْ سَأَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْهُ ذِكْرًا
ayahEnglish: And they ask you, [O Muḥammad], about Dhul-Qarnayn. Say, "I will recite to you about him a report."

targetTranslation: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in response to their question)
ayahArabic: وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَن ذِى ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ قُلْ سَأَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْهُ ذِكْرًا
ayahEnglish: And they ask you, [O Muḥammad], about Dhul-Qarnayn. Say, "I will recite to you about him a report."

ayahArabic: وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَن ذِى ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ قُلْ سَأَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْهُ ذِكْرًا
ayahEnglish: And they ask you, [O Muḥammad], about Dhul-Qarnayn. Say, "I will recite to you about him a report."

ayahArabic: إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَءَاتَيْنٰهُ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We established him upon the earth, and We gave him from everything a way [i.e., means].

ayahArabic: إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَءَاتَيْنٰهُ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We established him upon the earth, and We gave him from everything a way [i.e., means].

ayahArabic: إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَءَاتَيْنٰهُ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We established him upon the earth, and We gave him from everything a way [i.e., means].

targetTranslation: a means (object of آتَيْنٰهُ/We gave him)
ayahArabic: إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَءَاتَيْنٰهُ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We established him upon the earth, and We gave him from everything a way [i.e., means].

targetTranslation: a path/means (as the object being followed)
ayahArabic: فَأَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: So he followed a way

ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَغْرِبَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَغْرُبُ فِى عَيْنٍ حَمِئَةٍ وَوَجَدَ عِندَهَا قَوْمًا قُلْنَا يٰذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ إِمَّآ أَن تُعَذِّبَ وَإِمَّآ أَن تَتَّخِذَ فِيهِمْ حُسْنًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he reached the setting of the sun [i.e., the west], he found it [as if] setting in a body of dark water, and he found near it a people. We [i.e., Allāh] said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, either you punish [them] or else adopt among them [a way of] goodness."

targetTranslation: he reached (subject of وَجَدَ - he found)

targetTranslation: with goodness (describing how something will be done)

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَمَّا مَن ظَلَمَ فَسَوْفَ نُعَذِّبُهُۥ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ فَيُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "As for one who wrongs, we will punish him. Then he will be returned to his Lord, and He will punish him with a terrible punishment [i.e., Hellfire].

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَمَّا مَن ظَلَمَ فَسَوْفَ نُعَذِّبُهُۥ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ فَيُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "As for one who wrongs, we will punish him. Then he will be returned to his Lord, and He will punish him with a terrible punishment [i.e., Hellfire].

targetTranslation: a punishment (object of يُعَذِّبُ - he will punish)
ayahArabic: قَالَ أَمَّا مَن ظَلَمَ فَسَوْفَ نُعَذِّبُهُۥ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ فَيُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَمَّا مَن ظَلَمَ فَسَوْفَ نُعَذِّبُهُۥ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ فَيُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "As for one who wrongs, we will punish him. Then he will be returned to his Lord, and He will punish him with a terrible punishment [i.e., Hellfire].

ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا مَنْ ءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صٰلِحًا فَلَهُۥ جَزَآءً ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ وَسَنَقُولُ لَهُۥ مِنْ أَمْرِنَا يُسْرًا
ayahEnglish: But as for one who believes and does righteousness, he will have a reward of the best [i.e., Paradise], and we [i.e., Dhul-Qarnayn] will speak to him from our command with ease."

ayahArabic: وَأَمَّا مَنْ ءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صٰلِحًا فَلَهُۥ جَزَآءً ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ وَسَنَقُولُ لَهُۥ مِنْ أَمْرِنَا يُسْرًا

ayahEnglish: But as for one who believes and does righteousness, he will have a reward of the best [i.e., Paradise], and we [i.e., Dhul-Qarnayn] will speak to him from our command with ease."

ayahEnglish: But as for one who believes and does righteousness, he will have a reward of the best [i.e., Paradise], and we [i.e., Dhul-Qarnayn] will speak to him from our command with ease."

ayahArabic: ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Then he followed a way

targetTranslation: a way/means (object of أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
ayahArabic: ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Then he followed a way

ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

targetTranslation: he reached (مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ - the rising place of the sun)
ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

ayahEnglish: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

targetTranslation: we make/set (in response to لَّمْ before it)
ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

targetTranslation: below/beneath it (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

targetTranslation: a cover (object of وَجَدَهَا - he found it)
ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ وَقَدْ أَحَطْنَا بِمَا لَدَيْهِ خُبْرًا
ayahEnglish: Thus. And We had encompassed [all] that he had in knowledge.

targetTranslation: with him, near him (what was known about in خُبْرًا)
ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ وَقَدْ أَحَطْنَا بِمَا لَدَيْهِ خُبْرًا
ayahEnglish: Thus. And We had encompassed [all] that he had in knowledge.

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ وَقَدْ أَحَطْنَا بِمَا لَدَيْهِ خُبْرًا
ayahEnglish: Thus. And We had encompassed [all] that he had in knowledge.

ayahArabic: ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Then he followed a way.

targetTranslation: a path/means (object of أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
ayahArabic: ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا
ayahEnglish: Then he followed a way.

targetTranslation: when (showing a future or conditional time)
ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ بَيْنَ ٱلسَّدَّيْنِ وَجَدَ مِن دُونِهِمَا قَوْمًا لَّا يَكَادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ قَوْلًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he reached [a pass] between two mountains, he found beside them a people who could hardly understand [his] speech.

targetTranslation: he reached (connected to حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا - until when)

ayahEnglish: Until, when he reached [a pass] between two mountains, he found beside them a people who could hardly understand [his] speech.

targetTranslation: a saying (object of يَفْقَهُونَ - they understand)
ayahArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ بَيْنَ ٱلسَّدَّيْنِ وَجَدَ مِن دُونِهِمَا قَوْمًا لَّا يَكَادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ قَوْلًا
ayahEnglish: Until, when he reached [a pass] between two mountains, he found beside them a people who could hardly understand [his] speech.

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ يٰذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰٓ أَن تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا
ayahEnglish: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ يٰذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰٓ أَن تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا
ayahEnglish: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

targetTranslation: those who spread corruption (subject of يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ)

ayahEnglish: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

ayahEnglish: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

ayahEnglish: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ يٰذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰٓ أَن تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا
ayahEnglish: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ يٰذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰٓ أَن تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا
ayahEnglish: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ مَا مَكَّنِّى فِيهِ رَبِّى خَيْرٌ فَأَعِينُونِى بِقُوَّةٍ أَجْعَلْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ رَدْمًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "That in which my Lord has established me is better [than what you offer], but assist me with strength [i.e., manpower]; I will make between you and them a dam.

ayahArabic: قَالَ مَا مَكَّنِّى فِيهِ رَبِّى خَيْرٌ فَأَعِينُونِى بِقُوَّةٍ أَجْعَلْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ رَدْمًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "That in which my Lord has established me is better [than what you offer], but assist me with strength [i.e., manpower]; I will make between you and them a dam.

ayahArabic: قَالَ مَا مَكَّنِّى فِيهِ رَبِّى خَيْرٌ فَأَعِينُونِى بِقُوَّةٍ أَجْعَلْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ رَدْمًا
ayahEnglish: He said, "That in which my Lord has established me is better [than what you offer], but assist me with strength [i.e., manpower]; I will make between you and them a dam.

ayahEnglish: He said, "That in which my Lord has established me is better [than what you offer], but assist me with strength [i.e., manpower]; I will make between you and them a dam.

ayahArabic: ءَاتُونِى زُبَرَ ٱلْحَدِيدِ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا سَاوَىٰ بَيْنَ ٱلصَّدَفَيْنِ قَالَ ٱنفُخُوا۟ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَعَلَهُۥ نَارًا قَالَ ءَاتُونِىٓ أُفْرِغْ عَلَيْهِ قِطْرًا
ayahEnglish: Bring me bars of iron" - until, when he had leveled [them] between the two mountain walls, he said, "Blow [with bellows]," until when he had made it [like] fire, he said, "Bring me, that I may pour over it molten copper."

ayahArabic: ءَاتُونِى زُبَرَ ٱلْحَدِيدِ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا سَاوَىٰ بَيْنَ ٱلصَّدَفَيْنِ قَالَ ٱنفُخُوا۟ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَعَلَهُۥ نَارًا قَالَ ءَاتُونِىٓ أُفْرِغْ عَلَيْهِ قِطْرًا

ayahArabic: فَمَا ٱسْطٰعُوٓا۟ أَن يَظْهَرُوهُ وَمَا ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟ لَهُۥ نَقْبًا
ayahEnglish: So they [i.e., Gog and Magog] were unable to pass over it, nor were they able [to effect] in it any penetration.

ayahEnglish: So they [i.e., Gog and Magog] were unable to pass over it, nor were they able [to effect] in it any penetration.

ayahArabic: قَالَ هٰذَا رَحْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّى فَإِذَا جَآءَ وَعْدُ رَبِّى جَعَلَهُۥ دَكَّآءَ وَكَانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّى حَقًّا
ayahEnglish: [Dhul-Qarnayn] said, "This is a mercy from my Lord; but when the promise of my Lord comes [i.e., approaches], He will make it level, and ever is the promise of my Lord true."

ayahArabic: قَالَ هٰذَا رَحْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّى فَإِذَا جَآءَ وَعْدُ رَبِّى جَعَلَهُۥ دَكَّآءَ وَكَانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّى حَقًّا

ayahArabic: وَتَرَكْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَمُوجُ فِى بَعْضٍ وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَجَمَعْنٰهُمْ جَمْعًا
ayahEnglish: And We will leave them that day surging over each other, and [then] the Horn will be blown, and We will assemble them in [one] assembly.

ayahArabic: وَتَرَكْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَمُوجُ فِى بَعْضٍ وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَجَمَعْنٰهُمْ جَمْعًا

ayahArabic: وَعَرَضْنَا جَهَنَّمَ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْكٰفِرِينَ عَرْضًا
ayahEnglish: And We will present Hell that Day to the disbelievers, on display -

ayahArabic: وَعَرَضْنَا جَهَنَّمَ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْكٰفِرِينَ عَرْضًا
ayahEnglish: And We will present Hell that Day to the disbelievers, on display -

targetTranslation: as a display (emphasizing how we displayed Hell)

ayahEnglish: And We will present Hell that Day to the disbelievers, on display -

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ كَانَتْ أَعْيُنُهُمْ فِى غِطَآءٍ عَن ذِكْرِى وَكَانُوا۟ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَمْعًا
ayahEnglish: Those whose eyes had been within a cover [removed] from My remembrance, and they were not able to hear.

targetTranslation: do they then think? (questioning the thinking of الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟)
ayahArabic: أَفَحَسِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَن يَتَّخِذُوا۟ عِبَادِى مِن دُونِىٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ إِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكٰفِرِينَ نُزُلًا
ayahEnglish: Then do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants instead of Me as allies? Indeed, We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging.

ayahEnglish: Then do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants instead of Me as allies? Indeed, We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging.

targetTranslation: Hell (as the object of أَعْتَدْنَا - we have prepared)
ayahArabic: أَفَحَسِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَن يَتَّخِذُوا۟ عِبَادِى مِن دُونِىٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ إِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكٰفِرِينَ نُزُلًا
ayahEnglish: Then do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants instead of Me as allies? Indeed, We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging.

targetTranslation: as hospitality (what is prepared by اللَّهِ)
ayahArabic: أَفَحَسِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَن يَتَّخِذُوا۟ عِبَادِى مِن دُونِىٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ إِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكٰفِرِينَ نُزُلًا
ayahEnglish: Then do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants instead of Me as allies? Indeed, We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging.

ayahArabic: قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُم بِٱلْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمٰلًا
ayahEnglish: Say, [O Muḥammad], "Shall we [believers] inform you of the greatest losers as to [their] deeds?

ayahArabic: قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُم بِٱلْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمٰلًا
ayahEnglish: Say, [O Muḥammad], "Shall we [believers] inform you of the greatest losers as to [their] deeds?

targetTranslation: deeds/actions (as the thing being described in أَخْسَرِينَ/greatest losers)
ayahArabic: قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُم بِٱلْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمٰلًا
ayahEnglish: Say, [O Muḥammad], "Shall we [believers] inform you of the greatest losers as to [their] deeds?

targetTranslation: those who (subject of ضَلَّ - went astray)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا
ayahEnglish: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."

targetTranslation: he went completely astray (describes الَّذِينَ - those who)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا
ayahEnglish: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا
ayahEnglish: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا
ayahEnglish: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."

targetTranslation: they do well (thinking they are doing good deeds)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا
ayahEnglish: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا
ayahEnglish: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."

targetTranslation: those (pointing to something far) (subject of كَفَرُوا)
ayahArabic: أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَلِقَآئِهِۦ فَحَبِطَتْ أَعْمٰلُهُمْ فَلَا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ وَزْنًا
ayahEnglish: Those are the ones who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord and in [their] meeting Him, so their deeds have become worthless; and We will not assign to them on the Day of Resurrection any weight [i.e., importance].

targetTranslation: those who/the ones who (subject of كَفَرُوا)

ayahEnglish: Those are the ones who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord and in [their] meeting Him, so their deeds have become worthless; and We will not assign to them on the Day of Resurrection any weight [i.e., importance].

targetTranslation: that (pointing to something far) (subject of جَزَآؤُهُمْ)
ayahArabic: ذٰلِكَ جَزَآؤُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ بِمَا كَفَرُوا۟ وَٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ ءَايٰتِى وَرُسُلِى هُزُوًا
ayahEnglish: That is their recompense - Hell - for what they denied and [because] they took My signs and My messengers in ridicule.

ayahArabic: ذٰلِكَ جَزَآؤُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ بِمَا كَفَرُوا۟ وَٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ ءَايٰتِى وَرُسُلِى هُزُوًا

ayahEnglish: That is their recompense - Hell - for what they denied and [because] they took My signs and My messengers in ridicule.

targetTranslation: those who (referring to a group of people who ءَامَنُوا۟)
ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّالِحٰتِ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلًا
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - they will have the Gardens of Paradise as a lodging,

targetTranslation: those who remain (plural, in accusative case)
ayahArabic: خٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يَبْغُونَ عَنْهَا حِوَلًا
ayahEnglish: Wherein they abide eternally. They will not desire from it any transfer.

targetTranslation: they seek/desire (referring to the dwellers of Paradise)
ayahArabic: خٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يَبْغُونَ عَنْهَا حِوَلًا
ayahEnglish: Wherein they abide eternally. They will not desire from it any transfer.

targetTranslation: a change/alternative (object of يَبْغُونَ - they seek)
ayahArabic: خٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يَبْغُونَ عَنْهَا حِوَلًا
ayahEnglish: Wherein they abide eternally. They will not desire from it any transfer.

ayahArabic: قُل لَّوْ كَانَ ٱلْبَحْرُ مِدَادًا لِّكَلِمٰتِ رَبِّى لَنَفِدَ ٱلْبَحْرُ قَبْلَ أَن تَنفَدَ كَلِمٰتُ رَبِّى وَلَوْ جِئْنَا بِمِثْلِهِۦ مَدَدًا
ayahEnglish: Say, "If the sea were ink for [writing] the words of my Lord, the sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were exhausted, even if We brought the like of it in [continual] supplement."

targetTranslation: he/it was (describing the sea/ocean in this ayah)
ayahArabic: قُل لَّوْ كَانَ ٱلْبَحْرُ مِدَادًا لِّكَلِمٰتِ رَبِّى لَنَفِدَ ٱلْبَحْرُ قَبْلَ أَن تَنفَدَ كَلِمٰتُ رَبِّى وَلَوْ جِئْنَا بِمِثْلِهِۦ مَدَدًا

targetTranslation: as additional support/aid (object of جِئْنَا/we brought)
ayahArabic: قُل لَّوْ كَانَ ٱلْبَحْرُ مِدَادًا لِّكَلِمٰتِ رَبِّى لَنَفِدَ ٱلْبَحْرُ قَبْلَ أَن تَنفَدَ كَلِمٰتُ رَبِّى وَلَوْ جِئْنَا بِمِثْلِهِۦ مَدَدًا
ayahEnglish: Say, "If the sea were ink for [writing] the words of my Lord, the sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were exhausted, even if We brought the like of it in [continual] supplement."

ayahArabic: قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلٰهُكُمْ إِلٰهٌ وٰحِدٌ فَمَن كَانَ يَرْجُوا۟ لِقَآءَ رَبِّهِۦ فَلْيَعْمَلْ عَمَلًا صٰلِحًا وَلَا يُشْرِكْ بِعِبَادَةِ رَبِّهِۦٓ أَحَدًۢا
ayahEnglish: Say, "I am only a man like you, to whom has been revealed that your god is one God. So whoever would hope for the meeting with his Lord - let him do righteous work and not associate in the worship of his Lord anyone."
